key_evidence.claim_strength: Do not claim significant ROC-AUC superiority over baseline

key_evidence.evidence_topic: Paired SACU vs GradientBoosting ROC-AUC comparison

key_evidence.supporting_result: Default balanced accuracy = 0.6762; specificity improved from 0.9148 to 0.9336

key_evidence.claim_strength: Useful but specificity trade-off must be stated

key_evidence.supporting_result: Individual agents did not exceed learned SACU fusion in balanced accuracy.
key_evidence.claim_strength: Supports value of fusion over isolated agents
Use cautiously as equal-weight leave-one-out evidence

key_evidence.evidence_topic: Statistically supported SACU superiority over weaker agents
key_evidence.main_result: Primary SACU significantly exceeded: MultiViewAgent, TemporalSpatialAgent, MetadataAgent, AdaptiveControlAgent

recommended_tables.proposed_title: Clinical operating-point analysis of the learned SACU fusion
recommended_tables.main_content: Default, Youden, high-sensitivity, and high-specificity thresholds; balanced accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, NPV, F1, MCC
recommended_tables.reviewer_issue_addressed: Threshold selection, calibration-like operating behavior, clinical deployment interpretation

recommended_tables.proposed_title: Organizational role contribution and SACU ablation analysis
recommended_tables.main_content: Full learned fusion, equal-weight fusion, leave-one-agent-out conditions, individual agents, and role contribution loss
recommended_tables.reviewer_issue_addressed: Ablation evidence, role contribution, model component necessity

recommended_tables.proposed_title: Bootstrap confidence intervals for baseline, SACU fusion, and organizational agents
recommended_tables.main_content: ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, balanced accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1, MCC with 95% CIs
recommended_tables.reviewer_issue_addressed: Point estimates, uncertainty ranges, statistical reporting

recommended_tables.main_content: Primary SACU vs GradientBoosting baseline and agents; paired metric differences, 95% CIs, bootstrap p-values
recommended_tables.reviewer_issue_addressed: Statistical model comparison and fair interpretation of superiority claims

recommended_figures.proposed_title: Clinical ROC and precision–recall operating-point curves
recommended_figures.main_message: Learned SACU fusion has clinically interpretable threshold-dependent behavior.

recommended_figures.proposed_title: Bootstrap confidence intervals for ROC-AUC and balanced accuracy
recommended_figures.main_message: Uncertainty ranges show SACU is statistically comparable to the strongest baseline and stronger than weaker agents.
Bilateral and local-regional roles provide the clearest positive marginal contributions.

response_claims.reviewer_concern: Results were reported mainly as point estimates.
response_claims.response_claim: We added bootstrap 95% confidence intervals for ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, balanced accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1, and MCC.
response_claims.safe_language: The revised analysis reports uncertainty ranges rather than relying only on point estimates.

response_claims.response_claim: We added paired bootstrap comparisons between the learned SACU fusion, GradientBoosting baseline, fusion variants, and individual organizational agents.
response_claims.safe_language: The learned SACU fusion was statistically higher than weaker agents, while its ROC-AUC difference from GradientBoosting was not statistically significant.

response_claims.response_claim: We added a clinical operating-point analysis comparing default, Youden, high-sensitivity, and high-specificity thresholds.
response_claims.safe_language: Threshold optimization improved balanced operating behavior without changing the trained model.

response_claims.reviewer_concern: The contribution of organizational roles was insufficiently demonstrated.
response_claims.response_claim: We added individual-agent performance, leave-one-agent-out ablation, and role contribution loss analyses.
response_claims.safe_language: Bilateral and local-regional roles showed the clearest positive marginal signal; we avoid claiming that every role independently improves performance.

response_claims.reviewer_concern: Claims of superiority were too strong.
response_claims.response_claim: We revised the interpretation to distinguish discrimination, balanced operating behavior, and statistical significance.
response_claims.safe_language: The revised manuscript no longer claims universal state-of-the-art superiority.
